mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.2% signals a tightening labor market that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing starts rate above 1.3 million units signals an expansion in housing demand that boosts consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 102.5 signals a contraction in supply that reduces the availability of goods and services, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 2.5% signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts economic activity and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in the employment rate below 60% signals a contraction in labor demand that reduces the supply of labor, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real personal consumption expenditures rate above 2.5% signals an expansion in consumer demand that boosts economic activity and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips unexpectedly during a recession.
